better-sqlite3.interface: new Database(sharedPath); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); db.close()

shape.one_db_handle: src/db/client.ts opens ONE better-sqlite3 handle over the SHARED registry DB file and returns a drizzle DB typed over the UNION of the four upstream packages' schemas (one-db-handle). The compiler reads from agent-registry, agent-tool-registry, agent-provider, and agent-policy tables in a single connection.
shape.reopen_proves_cache: the compile-cache test closes + reopens the handle before the second compile to prove the composed_prompts row persisted (reopen-proves-cache).
shape.key_api_note: The compiler is a CONSUMER package — it writes only the composed_prompts cache; it reads the upstream tables. Gate tests on the runner exit code (never grep -q passed).

registry-consumed.source: packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composition-store.ts (CompositionStore, ResolvedComponent — lines 110-398, 41-50); packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composed-prompt-store.ts (ComposedPromptStore, contextHash, ComposedPrompt — lines 37-46, 67-75, 99-202); packages/ai/agent-registry/src/index.ts (barrel)

shape.component_model: Decision 5 head/version split: registry_components (identity, slug TEXT PK) + registry_component_versions (history, version_id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, UNIQUE(slug,version)). The old single registry_prompt_components (slug,version) composite-PK table no longer exists.
shape.resolveComposition: CompositionStore.resolveComposition(agentSlug, ctx?): ResolvedComponent[] — the compiler's resolve/composition.ts CALLS this and concatenates component.content in returned (position) order. ResolvedComponent = { componentSlug, position, resolvedVersion, component: PromptComponent }. component.version is the human version number; component.versionId is the registry_component_versions.version_id surrogate. The compiler MUST NOT re-implement ORDER BY position or the context evaluator (context-precedence-consumed) — that logic is OWNED by agent-registry (Decision 2). See packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composition-store.ts:243-297.
shape.version_pin: registry_agent_components.version_pin stores a registry_component_versions.version_id (enforced nullable FK), not a human version number. null = resolve latest at resolve-time. CompositionStore.attach() accepts versionPin (version_id) or pinVersion (human version → resolved to version_id).
shape.composed_prompts_component_versions: the JSON audit map {componentSlug: version} written to registry_composed_prompts.component_versions records human version numbers (not version_ids) — human-readable audit record per Decision 5. Use ComponentStore.resolveVersionId(slug,version) if a stable version_id is needed.
shape.contextHash: contextHash(context: Record<string,string>): string — takes only string values; sorted-key JSON canonicalization → SHA-256 hex. The compiler REUSES this exported helper for the cache key so agent-registry, agent-compiler, and agent-mcp all derive the SAME hash. Single source of truth. See packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composed-prompt-store.ts:37-46.
shape.ComposedPromptStore: ComposedPromptStore.write({agentSlug,contextHash,content,componentVersions}): ComposedPrompt — WRITTEN by the compiler (cache row insert). lookup(agentSlug,hash): ComposedPrompt|null — O(1) cache hit check before recompile. read(id): ComposedPrompt — by PK. ComposedPrompt.id is number (integer autoincrement). See packages/ai/agent-registry/src/store/composed-prompt-store.ts:115-181.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-compiler DEPENDS ON agent-registry-schema. References CompositionStore.resolveComposition + ComposedPromptStore.write/lookup + contextHash.

shape.resolveTools: the compiler's resolve/tools.ts reads AgentToolStore.listForAgent(agentSlug) and joins ToolBindingStore.resolve(toolName, platform) to produce ResolvedTool[] (canonical -> platform alias + availability); drops 'unavailable' bindings. shell_exec -> 'Bash' on claude_code, 'bash' on claude_api.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-compiler DEPENDS ON agent-tool-registry. References AgentToolStore.listForAgent + ToolBindingStore.resolve.
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by tool-header-emit. The markdown emitter renders the alias list as `tools: Read, Grep, WebSearch`; the json emitter renders the structured array.

shape.resolveModel: the compiler's resolve/model.ts reads the agent's model_hint and calls ModelStore.resolveModelId(modelHint, platform) -> platformModelId (claude_opus_4_8 -> 'opus' on claude_code, 'claude-opus-4-8' on claude_api); falls back to the canonical id if no binding (recorded in decisions.md).
shape.ToolFormatStore: the json emitter consults ToolFormatStore.getShape(providerId, canonicalTool) to shape tool defs per provider (custom vs server_side type-tagged) at COMPILE time, mirroring agent-provider's emitToolsForProvider branching.
shape.dependency_edge: agent-compiler DEPENDS ON agent-provider. References ModelStore.resolveModelId + ToolFormatStore.getShape. This is COMPILE-time model resolution — the reason the provider registry was extracted from agent-mcp (eliminates the cycle where the compiler would otherwise import the orchestrator).

shape.resolvePolicyConstraints: the compiler's resolve/policy.ts reads AgentPolicyStore.listForAgent(agentSlug) (direct + inherited via inherited_from), joins PolicyTemplateStore.read(policySlug).rules, and produces Constraint[] the header/body renders (no-credentials -> 'Never write API keys or secrets...'). Respects the eager/lazy inheritance decision OWNED by agent-policy (plan 4).
shape.AgentPolicyAssignment: consumes the AgentPolicyAssignment shape (cross-package type; to live in @adhd/agent-mcp-types per the shared-type rule).
shape.dependency_edge: agent-compiler DEPENDS ON agent-policy. References AgentPolicyStore.listForAgent + PolicyTemplateStore.read.
shape.key_api_note: Consumed by model-and-policy-emit. The compiler folds policy rows into the compiled permissions/constraint block.

agent-mcp-types-shared-additions.interface: ADD to @adhd/agent-mcp-types (per REFERENCES.md:197-211): ComposedPrompt, PromptComponent, AgentPolicyAssignment

shape.ComposedPrompt: { id: number; agentSlug: string; contextHash: string; content: string; componentVersions: Record<string, number>; createdAt: string } — id is number (integer autoincrement PK matching registry_composed_prompts); the compiler RETURNS this shape ([def:composed-output]) and writes it to both the registry composed_prompts cache and agent-mcp's runtime sink.
shape.PromptComponent: { slug: string; type: string; content: string; version: number; versionId: number; isShared: boolean; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string } — versionId added by Decision 5.
shape.AgentPolicyAssignment: { agentSlug: string; policySlug: string; overrideConfig: Record<string,unknown>|null; isMandatory: boolean; inheritedFrom: string|null } — produced by @adhd/agent-policy, consumed by the compiler.
shape.NEW_SHARED_TYPES: These three types are SHARED across registry packages + agent-mcp. Per REFERENCES.md:197-211 they must be ADDED to @adhd/agent-mcp-types/src/domain.ts (additive, no circular dep) rather than duplicated in a new types package. Do NOT create @adhd/agent-registry-types. (ProviderAdapter + StreamChunk are added by the agent-provider plan; ComposedPrompt + PromptComponent + AgentPolicyAssignment are added here / wherever first needed.)
shape.key_api_note: The compiler's compileAgent return value ({ id?, content, tools, componentVersions }) conforms to ComposedPrompt once the cache write assigns the id. agent-mcp (plan 6) consumes the same ComposedPrompt shape at session start.

agent-compiler-public-api.interface: @adhd/agent-compiler exports: { compileAgent } and a `bin` CLI (agent-registry compile <slug>)

shape.compileAgent: compileAgent({ agentSlug, platform, context, db }): Promise<{ id?: string; content: string; tools: ResolvedTool[]; componentVersions: Record<string,number> }> — THE convergence entrypoint. Orchestrates resolveBody (registry) + resolveTools (tool-registry) + resolveModel/ToolFormat (provider) + resolvePolicyConstraints (policy), dispatches on the platform header_format to emit/markdown.ts (yaml_frontmatter) or emit/json.ts (json_object), and writes/hits the composed_prompts cache (id set on cache write). claude_code -> markdown with --- frontmatter (name, description, tools: aliases, model: alias) + junction-ordered body + policy constraint block; claude_api -> { systemPrompt, tools }.
shape.cli_bin:
package.json bin maps `agent-registry` (compile --platform

--context '{...}' --format json --out-dir --all --category --db ) -> ./src/cli/compile.js. Default writes content to stdout; --out-dir writes .md; exits non-zero on unknown slug/platform. Mirrors USAGE.md 'Compiling to Markdown'.
shape.consumed_by_agent_mcp: @adhd/agent-mcp (plan 6) imports compileAgent for session-start prompt resolution (USAGE.md 'Runtime Integration via agent-mcp'); the migration (plan 7) drives the compile CLI as the round-trip equivalence gate.
shape.key_api_note: agent-compiler is the CONVERGENCE point: it DEPENDS ON all four foundation plans (agent-registry-schema, agent-tool-registry, agent-provider, agent-policy). It is the single package that joins all four domains' stores. Downstream: agent-mcp-refactor (plan 6) and agent-registry-migration (plan 7) depend on agent-compiler. No cycles (provider/types extraction guarantees this).
